author: codex
scope: Codex-only prep artifact for remaining MTA parent/department/person contamination after identity222. Focused on high-confidence alias_merge or do_not_merge decisions involving MTA parent, NYCT, MTA Bus, Access-a-Ride, MTA Corporate Compliance, MTA Real Estate, procurement office, Department of Buses, and Frank Annicaro/Farrell person contamination.

proposed_durable_decisions.reason: The first record is the parent Metropolitan Transportation Authority, while the second is New York City Transit / NYCT. NYCT may be part of or operate under MTA, but it is not the same entity.
proposed_durable_decisions.guard_notes: Do not let generic 'MTA' labels or MTA-NYCT joint project labels collapse parent MTA into NYCT. Use part_of, operator, publisher, or partner relations instead.

shared_keys_or_reasons: negative: service program vs parent transit authority

proposed_durable_decisions.reason: Access-a-Ride is a paratransit service program managed within the transit organization; parent MTA is the authority. Program ownership/management is not identity.
proposed_durable_decisions.guard_notes: Keep AAR/MyAAR/service-program aliases together, but remove generic parent-MTA aliases from the AAR identity surface.

shared_keys_or_reasons: AAR described as managed by DOB
MTA Bus Company is a transit agency division/company

source_canonical_evidence.quote: Paratransit service managed by MTA NYCT Department of Buses

proposed_durable_decisions.reason: AAR is a paratransit service/program; MTA Bus Company is a bus operating company/division. They are related in bus/paratransit governance but not the same identity.
proposed_durable_decisions.guard_notes: Prefer relations such as managed_by, part_of_agency, or related_to for AAR/DOB/MTA Bus context.

shared_keys_or_reasons: negative: office vs parent transit authority

source_canonical_evidence.display_name: Office of the Chief Procurement Officer

proposed_durable_decisions.reason: The Office of the Chief Procurement Officer is an MTA office/procurement function, not the parent authority itself.
proposed_durable_decisions.guard_notes: Do not merge officer/office/procurement-department aliases into parent MTA. Preserve office-to-parent relations separately.

source_canonical_evidence.quote: Additional copies may be obtained from MTA Corporate Compliance

proposed_durable_decisions.reason: Both records name the same MTA Corporate Compliance office/department, with matching identity key entity_mta-corporate-compliance and evidence of the same compliance function.
proposed_durable_decisions.guard_notes: Alias only these Corporate Compliance office/department records. Do not merge Corporate Compliance into parent MTA or into the individual Chief Compliance Officer.

shared_keys_or_reasons: negative: office vs parent transit authority

source_canonical_evidence.quote: reach out to MTA Corporate Compliance for guidance

proposed_durable_decisions.reason: Corporate Compliance is a named MTA compliance office/department. It should not be identified as the whole parent authority.
proposed_durable_decisions.guard_notes: Represent Corporate Compliance as an internal office of MTA, not as an alias of MTA.

source_canonical_evidence.quote: MTA Real Estate will provide an annual report on leasing and construction opportunities

proposed_durable_decisions.reason: Both records refer to the same MTA Real Estate / Real Estate Department identity. The 167646 record is a narrow source-specific instance of the same department.
proposed_durable_decisions.guard_notes: Alias MTA Real Estate Department into MTA Real Estate, but do not merge it into parent MTA or into its subgroups such as TOM/RED or Transit-Oriented Development unless independently reviewed.

shared_keys_or_reasons: MTA Real Estate aliases include generic entity_mta
negative: agency department vs parent transit authority

source_canonical_evidence.quote: MTA Real Estate will provide an annual report

proposed_durable_decisions.reason: MTA Real Estate is an internal real-estate department/function, not the whole Metropolitan Transportation Authority.
proposed_durable_decisions.guard_notes: Strip or quarantine generic entity_mta aliases on MTA Real Estate records; preserve department-to-parent relation.

shared_keys_or_reasons: Frank Annicaro record carries Department of Buses role labels

source_canonical_evidence.display_name: Frank Annicaro, Senior Vice President, Buses, NYCT

source_canonical_evidence.quote: Requesting Department: Department of Buses, Frank Annicaro

proposed_durable_decisions.reason: Frank Annicaro is a person/executive associated with the Department of Buses. The department is the organizational unit; the person is not the same entity.
proposed_durable_decisions.guard_notes: Role-title aliases such as 'SVP Buses' can stay on the person only when the personal name is present. Department-only DOB aliases belong on Department of Buses.

shared_keys_or_reasons: both records are Department of Buses executives

source_canonical_evidence.display_name: Frank Annicaro, Senior Vice President, Buses, NYCT

source_canonical_evidence.quote: MTA Acting Executive Vice President of Department of Buses/MTA Bus Company

proposed_durable_decisions.reason: Frank Annicaro and Frank Farrell are different people who both appear in Department of Buses/MTA Bus leadership contexts.
proposed_durable_decisions.guard_notes: Do not merge people based on first name, shared DOB roles, or shared bus-executive titles. Keep person-to-department employment/leadership relations separate.

skipped_review_only.reason: Both appear to be parent-MTA records, but both clusters carry contamination from people, officers, committees, and subsidiary/department labels. Prefer a later parent-MTA alias-hygiene pass before a durable alias_merge.

AAR is described as managed by NYCT Department of Buses and is likely not NYCT itself, but the AAR cluster carries generic MTA/NYCT management labels. Leave for a dedicated AAR/DOB service-program cleanup.
Frank Annicaro is a person and MTA Bus Company is an organization, so DNM is likely, but this artifact already covers the clearest person-vs-department and person-vs-person guardrails.

commands_run: rg -n "entity_meeting-doc-64066-mta|entity_mta-entity-update-2025|entity_mta-nyct|entity_meeting-doc-154986-aar|entity_mta-chief-procurement-officer|entity_meeting-doc-135596-mta-corporate-compliance|entity_167646-mta-real-estate|entity_mta-real-estate|entity_frank-annicaro|Frank Farrell|frank-farrell|Farrell" data/identity-review data/canonical wiki/entities wiki/sources | head -300

jq -c 'select((.record_id|test("mta-real-estate|frank-annicaro|frank-farrell|mta-chief-procurement|corporate-compliance|meeting-doc-64066-mta|meeting-doc-154986-aar|mta-nyct|mta-entity-update-2025|mta-bus-company|department-of-buses")) or ((.display_name//"")|test("Frank Farrell|Frank Annicaro|MTA Real Estate|Corporate Compliance|Chief Procurement|Access-A-Ride|MTA Bus Company|Department of Buses|New York City Transit|Metropolitan Transportation Authority";"i"))) | {record_id, display_name, type:.payload.entity_type, evidence:(.evidence_refs[0].evidence_id), quote:(.evidence_refs[0].quote // .evidence_refs[0].source_quote // .evidence_refs[0].text)}' data/canonical/entities.jsonl | head -120
